name: Dong-Hyun Lee
education: B.S., School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering from Dankook University, Jukjeon, South Korea, (2018.03 ~ 2024.02)
M.S., School of Electrical Engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea, (2024.03 ~ 2026.02)
publication: S.-U. Kang*, D.-H. Lee*, M.-G. Song, M. Seok, and M.-S. Choo., IGA-SRAM: A Compact SRAM-based IMC Engine for Binary/Ternary Deep Neural Networks with ADC-Less and Error-Aware Training Algorithm, IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems (JETCAS), 2026(*Equally Credited Authors).
biography: Dong-Hyun Lee received a B.S. degree in the School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering from Dankook University, Jukjeon, South Korea, and the M.S. degree in the School of Electrical Engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea. His research interests include artificial intelligence hardware design and processing in memory (PIM).

name: Sang-Hyun Ok
education: B.S., School of Electrical Engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea, (2009.03 ~ 2016.02)
M.S., School of Electrical Engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea, (2024.03 ~ 2026.02)
publication: S.-H. Ok*, J.-G. Lee*, K.-H Lee, Haram Ju, G.-S Jeong, W. Bae, M.-S Choo, Critical-Timing-Relaxed pre-Decision Feedforward Equalizer for High-Speed DSP-based Wireline Receiver using Hardware-in-the-Loop Verification on RF-SoC, in 2025 IEEE Asian Solid-State Circuits Conference (A-SSCC), (*Equally Credited Authors).

S.-H. Ok, D.-H. Heo, J.-G. Lee, S.-M. Jin, K.-H. Lee, D. Jeong, and M.-S. Choo, An Implementation of a DSP-Based PAM-4 Digital Equalizer with 32-Way TI-ADC, in 2025 International Conference on Electronics, Information, and Communication (ICEIC).
Awards: ICEIC'25 Best Paper Award - Silver Prize
biography: Sang-Hyun Ok received the B.S. degree in the school of electronics and communication engineering at Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea, and the M.S. degree in electrical engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea. He joined LX Semicon, Seoul, South Korea in 2016, where he has been involved in the LCD DDI team. His research interests include digital and analog circuit design of Clock and Data Recovery (CDR) and digital circuit designs of PAM4 Receiver with adaptive decision feedback equalizer.

education: B.S., School of Electrical Engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea, (2017.03 ~ 2023.02)

Awards: ICEIC'24 Best Paper Award - Gold Prize
biography: Dong-Hoe Heo received the B.S. and M.S. degrees in the school of electrical engineering at Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea. His research interests include clock and data recovery (CDR) circuit design.

biography: Kang-Min Kim received the B.S. and M.S. degrees in the school of electrical engineering at Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea. His research interests include hardware security.

name: Kyu-Ran Park
education: M.S., School of Electrical Engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea, (2023.03 ~ 2025.02)
biography: Kyu-Ran Park received a B.S. degree in electrical engineering from Cheongju University, Cheongju, South Korea, and the M.S. degree in electrical engineering from Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea. She joined LX Semicon, Seoul, South Korea, in 2015, where she has been involved in the YPD team. Her research interests include analog and digital circuit designs of phase-locked-loop (PLL).

biography: In-Woo Jang received a B.S. degree in the school of electrical engineering at Hanyang University, Ansan, South Korea.
